title: zanthoxylum hawaiiense var. subacutum
intro: Zanthoxylum hawaiiense var. subacutum, also known as 'ili'okea, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, native to Hawaii. It has been traditionally used in Hawaiian medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating various health issues. The health benefits of 'ili'okea include relief from pain, fever reduction, and the treatment of respiratory and digestive issues. 'Ili'okea is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and salves, where it is combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects and promote overall well-being.
